title: BA Zone — Discovery Findings
description: Structured output of the Ask-Why elicitation workflow by Phúc NT / BA Zone. Every field is required so that downstream BRD/PRD/SRS writers have a complete contract.
author: Phúc NT — Founder, BA Zone

business_problem.description: One-paragraph statement of the underlying business problem (NOT the proposed solution).

business_goal.description: Measurable goal that solving the problem will achieve. Include KPI + target + deadline where possible.

stakeholders.description: Who is affected, who decides, who pays, who operates. Include role + organisation, not just names.

pain_points.description: Observable symptoms reported by stakeholders today.

root_causes.description: Why the pain exists. Must be one level deeper than pain_points (apply 5-Whys).

functional_requirements.description: What the system must DO. Each item must be actionable for a designer or developer.

non_functional_requirements.description: How well the system must do it — performance, availability, security, scalability, compliance.

business_rules.description: Domain rules, thresholds, eligibility, validation logic. Each rule must be testable.

constraints.description: Hard limits: regulatory, budget, timeline, technology stack, vendor lock-in.

risks.description: What could go wrong. Include likelihood/impact hint where possible.

assumptions.description: Things taken as true without explicit confirmation. Critical to surface for validation.

dependencies.description: External systems, teams, regulations, or upstream decisions this work depends on.

open_questions.description: What is still unknown and who needs to answer it. Never leave empty unless discovery is truly complete.
